data.title: Review agent cannot write reviewer-findings.json to sub-agent worktree (isolation hook blocks)
data.description:
## Observed
When the orchestrator dispatches a code-reviewer sub-agent with a sub-agent worktree as the review target, the reviewer cannot write `reviewer-findings.json` to that worktree path. The main-repo isolation hook blocks writes to `REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/agent-*/`.

Result: `REVIEW_RESULT: failed` is returned but the stale reviewer-findings.json from a prior session remains on disk. The pre-commit review gate then reads the stale file, causing incorrect review status at commit time.

## Impact
Per-worktree commit workflow breaks for sub-agent worktrees when the orchestrator tries to dispatch the reviewer. Workaround was to resume the original sub-agent and have it run the full commit workflow in its own context — doubling token usage.

## Root Cause
Same as the Edit-tool block: sub-agent worktrees live inside REPO_ROOT, so the isolation hook treats them as main-repo files. The reviewer agent runs in a context that sees the worktree as off-limits.

## Expected
The reviewer should be able to write `reviewer-findings.json` to the worktree being reviewed. Alternatively, the sprint skill should always dispatch commit/review from within the sub-agent's own context rather than from the orchestrator.

## Fix Direction
Option A: Allowlist `REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/` in the write-blocking hook (same fix as 23f6-2150).
Option B: The sprint per-worktree-review-commit prompt should explicitly instruct sub-agents to run their own commit workflow before returning to the orchestrator, making cross-worktree review dispatch unnecessary.
